input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues here:

## 1. `StaticResource` doesn't update at runtime

`StaticResource` resolves **once** at XAML load time. To react to dictionary swaps, use **`DynamicResource`**:

```xml
<!-- ❌ Won't update -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryText}" />

<!-- ✅ Updates when the dictionary changes -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryText}" />
```

Then swap your merged dictionary at runtime:

```csharp
var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDicts.Clear();
mergedDicts.Add(new DarkTheme()); // or LightTheme
```

## 2. Activity restarts on Android UI mode change

Android destroys and recreates the Activity when `uiMode` changes. Prevent this by adding `UiMode` to `ConfigurationChanges` in your `MainActivity.cs`:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
        | ConfigChanges.Orientation
        | ConfigChanges.UiMode,  // ← add this
    LaunchMode = LaunchMode.SingleTop)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

With this, Android notifies instead of restarting. You can then detect the system change:

```csharp
// In App.xaml.cs or a service
Application.Current.RequestedThemeChanged += (s, e) =>
{
    // e.RequestedTheme == AppTheme.Dark or Light
    ApplyTheme(e.RequestedTheme);
};
```

**TL;DR:** `StaticResource` → `DynamicResource` for runtime updates; add `ConfigChanges.UiMode` to stop the restart.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about two issues with dark mode in their .NET MAUI Android app:

1. **StaticResource doesn't update at runtime** — This is a common mistake. `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and never updates. They need to use `DynamicResource` instead, which listens for changes to the resource dictionary.

2. **App restarts when Android dark mode is toggled** — By default, Android recreates the Activity when the UI mode (dark/light) changes. In .NET MAUI, you need to handle the `configChanges` attribute in the Android manifest (or via the Activity attribute) to include `uiMode` so the activity isn't recreated.


I'll walk through both solutions with the specific code changes needed to fix these issues.
